summary: Per-clinic session cookies must use standard cookie-domain scoping (host equals or is a subdomain of the cookie domain, registrable name only), user-entered wall-clock times must be compared in local time against Jane's absolute appointment times, and an unrecognized appointments response shape must hard-error rather than read as empty (an empty list would let conflict-check report a clinic as clear).
reason: Jane keeps each clinic on its own subdomain with a separate session, so cookie-domain matching must reject a cookie scoped to another clinic (leahkangas.janeapp.com) or a look-alike host (janeapp.com.attacker.example) — the prior match had a reversed suffix clause. conflict-check/watch compare a naive --at against appointment times; naive inputs on BOTH sides (the candidate in parseFlexibleDate and naive appointment strings in extractTimeFromKeys) are anchored to time.Local, while offset-carrying appointment strings stay absolute, so the comparison is like-for-like and can't shift by the local offset and miss a real overlap. A non-empty appointments object with an unknown wrapper key — or a recognized key (appointments/data/results) whose value is not an array — would report zero appointments and hide bookings/conflicts, so the fetch now returns an error instead of an empty list so safety checks don't run on incomplete data. apptEndKeys widened so the 60m end fallback fires even less often.
validated_outcome: cookieDomainMatches accepts exact/parent/host-only cookies and rejects cross-clinic, look-alike-host, and bare-TLD domains (table test). go build/vet/test and verify-skill pass.
